signing_authority: Not completed — yellow placeholder 'Your Name Here' in management assertion

system_description.overview: Reftab is asset management software designed for simplicity. It enables organizations to track and manage physical and digital assets. The platform includes automated compliance management and AI-powered security features.

products.Reftab Asset Management: Core SaaS platform for asset tracking and management
products.Automated Compliance Management: Module for compliance tracking and reporting
products.AI Security Features: AI-powered security capabilities (exact nature not detailed)

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — physical security delegated to AWS as subservice organization)
regions: Not explicitly named — implied US regions based on context
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices; fully cloud-hosted on AWS
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ — multiple availability zones configured for high availability

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with ACLs to control traffic between network segments; network segmentation separates systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality
network_architecture.firewalls: Network firewalls implemented, configured, and managed to control incoming/outgoing traffic based on predetermined security rules; firewall access restricted to authorized personnel; configurations reviewed regularly (CC6.6.1–CC6.6.3)
network_architecture.ids_ips: Intrusion detection system monitors and detects unauthorized access or anomalies within the network (CC2.1.1); formal network diagram outlining boundary protection including IDS maintained and reviewed annually (CC2.3.7)
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host used for remote/VPN access to production environment
network_architecture.tls: TLS used for data in transit; certificates from established CA
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic enters segmented VPCs; ACLs control inter-segment traffic; bastion host for admin access; IDS monitors network; firewalls control perimeter; production access restricted to authorized personnel

compute: EC2 or equivalent (implied — 'multiple servers across all availability zones in AWS')
application_architecture.environments: Segregated environments — development/test separated from production; change management enforces segregation (CC8.1.2)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Not named — generic access control described
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all remote access to entity's information systems (CC6.1.1)
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Promptly upon termination — exact SLA not stated (CC6.1.8)
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Policy and procedures established, documented, and enforced for password strength and confidentiality (CC6.1.5)

encryption.at_rest: Data encryption at rest implemented to protect sensitive information (CC6.1.10)
encryption.key_management: Access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only (CC6.1.9); cloud KMS (AWS) implied

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based — 'source code management tool' referenced; source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author (CC5.2.5)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Approval process required prior to merging to master branch; developers cannot make production code changes without additional approval (CC8.1.2, CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily (CC7.5.1); vulnerability scans conducted regularly (CC7.1.4)

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named — 'monitoring tool generates alerts when specific predefined thresholds are met' (CC5.2.1)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Log management processes established; logging enabled for administrative activities, log-on attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions, roles (CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events; incidents tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure (IaaS) — subservice organization under carve-out method
Third-party agreements established and reviewed for security compliance (CC9.2.1, CC9.2.2)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-malware technology implemented, maintained, and regularly updated on production servers and workstations (CC6.8.1)
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Regular vulnerability scans; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance
security_tools.penetration_testing: Not explicitly mentioned in controls tested — vulnerability and system monitoring procedures are established (CC7.1.2)

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to people/roles during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance effectiveness could not be tested (no cyber incidents during period)
CC7.3.1 — Incident response could not be tested (no security incidents during period)
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion could not be tested (no customer terminations during period)
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical facility access controls excluded (AWS responsibility as subservice org); SOC 2 report of AWS reviewed annually
A1.2 — Environmental protections excluded (no physical office; AWS responsibility)
notable_controls: RGEC (Risk and Governance Executive Committee) oversees security governance; RGEC Charter reviewed and approved annually; committee meets quarterly
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) governed by charter; meets weekly on security and operations
Risk assessments conducted periodically covering security, fraud, legal, regulatory, economic, competition, customer, vendor, technology, and information security
Formal network diagram with boundary protection (firewalls, IDS) maintained and reviewed annually (CC2.3.7)
Asset inventory maintained with details on asset ownership and location; reviewed on as-needed basis (CC2.1.4)
Data classification policy established and implemented (CC2.1.3)
Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author (CC5.2.5)
Production DB access restricted to authorized personnel based on roles (CC5.2.3)
DR plan covers roles/responsibilities, critical IT programs, OS, personnel, data files, time frames via BIA
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerts for backup failures (CC7.4.2)

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ with daily backups and failover confirmed. Single region (implied). VPC with segmentation. No CDN, no secondary cloud. Solid single-region setup but no multi-region redundancy confirmed.

application_architecture.rationale: No languages, frameworks, compute types, or API style disclosed. Separate environments and branch protection confirmed. Very limited technical disclosure.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies in place. No database names, caching layer, or data warehouse disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: IDS, antivirus/anti-malware, firewalls, regular vuln scans, MFA, cybersecurity insurance, access controls all confirmed. No WAF named. No pen test explicitly mentioned in tested controls. Solid controls breadth via ACCORP template.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, emergency change process, separate environments, and patch management confirmed. No CI/CD tool named, no container orchestration, no IaC tool named. Monthly patch reviews plus automatic patch management is a positive.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy tested annually, multi-AZ failover, daily backups with alerts. DR plan includes BIA. No multi-region failover. No RTO/RPO disclosed. Reftab's DR explicitly covers AZ-level failures only.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a specific vendor. Vendor management program exists but no other vendors named. High concentration on single cloud provider.

overall.rationale: SOC 2 Type 2 with zero exceptions across 49 controls is a meaningful positive — operating effectiveness tested over 3 months. However, this is an ACCORP PARTNERS template report with minimal company-specific technical disclosure. No tool names, no technology stack specifics. Asset management software with AI features described but no AI-specific security controls. Controls are adequate for the product stage.

red_flags: Management assertion signing authority field left blank ('Your Name Here' yellow placeholder) — report appears not fully finalized
Network diagram on page 19 is a 'Sample network diagram' placeholder watermark — actual Reftab architecture not shown
No penetration testing control explicitly listed in the tested controls section (unlike CoPlane) — notable gap for a Type 2 report
Zero specific technology names beyond AWS — no database, CI/CD, monitoring, or identity provider named
yellow_flags: Single-region architecture (AZ failover only, not multi-region) — DR is limited to AWS AZ-level failures
No WAF mentioned for a web-facing SaaS application
Access revocation SLA is 'promptly' — no specific timeframe stated (unlike CoPlane's 1 business day)
3-month Type 2 period (April 9 – July 9) is the minimum; longer periods provide more assurance
AI Security Features described but no AI-specific controls in scope
green_flags: SOC 2 Type 2 with zero exceptions across all 49 controls tested — operating effectiveness confirmed
Anti-malware on production servers and workstations confirmed and tested with sample evidence
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and automated alerts for failures
Cybersecurity insurance in place and tested
MFA enforced for all remote access
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly — active security governance
Risk assessments cover broad threat categories including vendor relationships and technology changes
Annual review of AWS SOC 2 report to assess subservice org controls
Data classification policy established and implemented
Separate environments with enforced change management controls
Branch protection with approval required before merging to master; emergency change process in place
key_observations: This is an ACCORP PARTNERS templated SOC 2 Type 2 report — the controls language is near-identical boilerplate shared with CoPlane (same auditor, same template); almost no company-specific technical detail was added
The placeholder network diagram and unsigned management assertion are significant quality issues for a completed report — suggests the document prep process was rushed
Reftab's DR is explicitly limited to AZ-level failures within AWS only — no multi-region DR capability described
The product is described as having AI Security Features but no AI-specific controls appear anywhere in the report — unclear what the AI capabilities actually are
Asset management software is typically used in enterprise environments; the lack of tenant data segregation disclosure is notable for a multi-tenant SaaS product
Vendor management program (CC9) is confirmed but no third-party vendor names are disclosed — cannot assess concentration risk beyond AWS
